title: Clinical Protocol Changes After Temporal Checkpoint
scenario: A clinical action passes an earlier temporal checkpoint, but a protocol update changes timing requirements before execution.
initial_state: The action satisfied the prior timing requirement at checkpoint.
state_transition: The applicable timing protocol changes before execution.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated under the current temporal protocol before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Temporal admissibility must be evaluated against the currently applicable execution-time timing rule.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because prior temporal compliance does not preserve admissibility after timing requirements change.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when current temporal protocol conditions are not satisfied.
clinical_action: Execute action under outdated temporal checkpoint
